stances.claim: Before her husband's presidency, CJ already knew of First Lady Louise Araneta-Marcos's competence as practicing lawyer and law professor â€” and was 'even more impressed' when she dissolved her entrenched law firm before the new president assumed office.
On two formal occasions in 2023 (June 28 Sistine Chapel exhibit; Aug 22 Toyota Philippines 35th anniversary), she 'acted so spontaneously as if we had known each other for years' â€” a real charmer.
Invited through compaÃ±ero Mike Toledo for Aug 19, the First Lady sent a personalized video citing 'sacred' days and requested a reschedule â€” and the awards were happily postponed to Sept 2.

Three groups of awardees will receive prizes from the First Lady, MVP, and CJ as FLP chair: 20 law scholars at P200,000 each (TYKF partnership represented by Lucio Tan III), five MBA Esmel fellows at P450,000 each (Metro Pacific partnership), and 20 dissertation winners (Ayala Corp partnership; First Prize P320,000 to Rachel Gella USLS, Second P220,000 to Arvin Maceda FEU, three Third P120,000 each, fifteen finalists P20,000 each).
Each Esmel fellowship is structured as P350,000 maximum tuition plus P100,000 books-and-living-allowance stipend, and the five winning dissertations can be accessed at www.libpros.com.
UP Law Dean Darlene Marie Berberabe is being inducted into the FLP Professorial Chairs program by FLP partners Metrobank chair Arthur Ty and Toyota Philippines chair Alfred Ty.

In its second decade (2021-2031), FLP aims to set up an immersive, interactive, AI-powered, tech-driven Center for Liberty and Prosperity (in SC partnership) hosting a Museum for Liberty and Prosperity, and a multi-billion-peso Prosperity Fund to help eliminate extreme poverty by investing in MSME equity and helping them help themselves through Esmel-fellow management expertise.

While other firms engage in microfinancing, FLP is â€” to its knowledge â€” the only entity aspiring to invest in and help manage MSMEs, with the goal of making them 'more credit-worthy and growth-worthy.'

notable_anecdotes.summary: The First-Lady-as-charmer credentialing â€” 'INDEED, A REAL CHARMER, FIRST LADY LOUISE ARANETA-MARCOS IS' â€” built on the two pre-presidency 2023 encounters with specific dates (June 28 Sistine Chapel reproductions; Aug 22 Toyota Philippines 35th anniversary) and the 'holding on to my arm occasionally' detail.
The personalized-video reply citing 'sacred' days â€” used as both a Catholic-piety signal and a polite-rescheduling explanation, leading to the column's Sept 2 rescheduled awards ceremony framing.
Lucio Tan III's billing â€” 'the Stanford summa, president of PAL Holdings and LT Group' â€” quietly elevating the next-generation TYKF representative.

Tetangco's bracketed elevation â€” 'past Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas governor Amando M. Tetangco Jr., now chair of the largest conglomerate in our country, SM Investments Corp.' â€” a single line that maps the FLP boardroom to corporate Philippines's apex.
Comprehensive name-by-name enumeration of all 20 scholars, 5 fellows, 20 dissertation winners (5 prize + 15 finalist), faculty advisers, and BOJs â€” turning the column into a permanent FLP record.
Dean Darlene Marie Berberabe's FLP Professorial Chair induction by Metrobank's Arthur Ty and Toyota Philippines' Alfred Ty â€” connecting the Ty family conglomerate dual-anchor to UP Law.
The www.libpros.com pointer for accessing winning dissertations â€” concrete public-access detail that makes the FLP prize program reviewable beyond the awards-night audience.
The Esmel fellowship breakdown (P350K tuition + P100K stipend = P450K total) â€” operational transparency on how the MBA-for-MSME-management pipeline is funded.

The Sandoval-Gutierrez-led FLP Board of Trustees roster â€” nine names including two retired justices, a former Education Secretary, and a former BSP governor â€” establishing FLP's institutional weight.

The second-decade strategic announcement: AI-powered Center for Liberty and Prosperity (with SC partnership), Museum for Liberty and Prosperity, and multi-billion-peso Prosperity Fund for MSME equity investment â€” paired with the closing positioning that 'FLP is the only entity aspiring to invest in and help manage MSMEs.'

one_paragraph_summary: Panganiban prefaces today's FLP awards ceremony with a portrait of First Lady Louise Araneta-Marcos â€” whose competence as practicing lawyer and law professor he had heard of before her husband's presidency, and whom he met formally on June 28, 2023 (Sistine Chapel reproductions exhibit) and Aug 22, 2023 (Toyota Philippines 35th anniversary), where she acted so spontaneously, occasionally holding his arm, as if they had known each other for years: 'INDEED, A REAL CHARMER.' Invited through compaÃ±ero Mike Toledo (Chamber of Mines chair and president) for Aug 19, she sent a personalized video citing 'sacred' days and requested a reschedule to today, Sept 2. Three groups of awardees will receive prizes from her, guest speaker Manuel V. Pangilinan (MVP), and CJ as FLP chair: 20 law scholars at P200,000 each (Tan Yan Kee Foundation partnership represented by Lucio Tan III, 'the Stanford summa, president of PAL Holdings and LT Group'), with 10 third-year students (Alberto, Despi, Mirasol, Mutuc, Orajay, Paguia, Sy, SobreviÃ±as, Te, Viernes) and 10 fourth-year students (Barraquias, Cerillo, David, Gruyal, Laza, Patricio, Aimiel and Allan Reyes, Salcedo, Sanders Jr.) named with their schools; five MBA Esmel fellows at P450,000 each (Metro Pacific Investments Foundation partnership; P350,000 tuition + P100,000 stipend) â€” Ballestar, Lucas, Panlilio II, Tuquero, and Villanueva; and 20 dissertation contest winners (Ayala Corp partnership) â€” Rachel Gella (USLS) first prize P320,000, Arvin Maceda (FEU) second P220,000, three third placers at P120,000 (De Guia, Loro, Quebrar), and 15 finalists at P20,000 each, with the five winning dissertations accessible at www.libpros.com. The three boards of judges were chaired by CJ Alexander G. Gesmundo (scholars), past BSP governor Amando M. Tetangco Jr. â€” 'now chair of the largest conglomerate in our country, SM Investments Corp.' (Esmel fellows) â€” and Justice Amy C. Lazaro-Javier (dissertations). UP Law Dean Darlene Marie Berberabe will be inducted into the FLP Professorial Chairs program by Metrobank chair Arthur Ty and Toyota Philippines chair Alfred Ty. The FLP Board of Trustees comprises retired Justice Angelina Sandoval-Gutierrez (president), retired Justice Estela M. Perlas-Bernabe, former Education Secretary Edilberto de Jesus, Tetangco, Maria Elena P. Yaptangco, professor Maria Theresa P. MaÃ±alac, lawyers Joel Emerson Gregorio and Arvin Paolo D. Cortez, and CJ as chairperson. In its second decade (2021-2031), FLP aims to set up an immersive, interactive, AI-powered, tech-driven Center for Liberty and Prosperity (in partnership with the Supreme Court) hosting a Museum for Liberty and Prosperity, and a multi-billion-peso Prosperity Fund to help eliminate extreme poverty by investing in MSME equity and helping them through Esmel-fellow management expertise â€” unleashing their entrepreneurial ingenuity. 'To our knowledge, while there are firms engaged in microfinancing, FLP is the only entity aspiring to invest in and help manage MSMEs' â€” making them 'more credit-worthy and growth-worthy.'
